description_long: This object consists of a group of rustic cowsheds and open shelters, constructed primarily from weathered wooden planks. The buildings feature simple, gable-style roofs with visible gaps and missing planks, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The structures vary in size, with some larger barns and smaller outbuildings, as well as open-sided shelters supported by wooden posts. The walls are made of vertical wooden boards, and some buildings have large open entrances or doorways, while others are more enclosed. There are no visible windows, but the gaps in the roofs and walls provide natural ventilation. The overall appearance is utilitarian and functional, typical of traditional rural agricultural architecture.
description: A group of weathered wooden cowsheds and open shelters with gable roofs, open entrances, and rustic construction. The structures are aged, utilitarian, and lack windows, with visible gaps for ventilation.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the front and side of the largest cowshed, with its wide entrance and adjacent smaller sheds and open shelters.
description_view_1: View 1 highlights the rear and side profiles of the buildings, revealing the open framework of the shelter and the solid backs of the sheds.
description_view_2: View 2 provides a perspective from the opposite side, showing the arrangement of the buildings and the open-sided shelter in the foreground.
description_view_3: View 3 displays the backs of the main cowsheds, emphasizing the gable roofs and the lack of windows or decorative features.
attribute_window_presence: no windows, open gaps in walls and roofs
attribute_ventilation_features: open eaves, gaps in roof and walls
